style_stack.visual: broll_montage PRIMARY (matrix=static plates+hard cuts; smoke keeps Ken Burns)

beats.speakable_text: If a hot face is familiar, this shame pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun shame usually makes the loop more expensive. More hiding, more over-explaining. And less chance anyone can actually help.
Shame often persists because the nervous system treats being seen clearly as a threat to belonging. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

Try this. Name the actual event out loud in one sentence, set it apart from your worth in a second sentence. And choose one honest next step. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. The mistake is information about a moment, not a verdict on who you are.
